evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 2 executed exactly the roadmap from iter-1 memory: (1) voice carry-through near Execution Instructions + Reflection-Loop voice-audit bullet (agent.py), and (2) threaded webpage_context + allowed_competitors through run_review with _check_evidence_grounding and _check_competitor_leak post-filters in gemini.py, plus service.py wiring. Both are well-engineered: the grounding check has a proper unobservability escape hatch, the competitor leak detector is grammatical (not hardcoded brand list) with a thoughtful _PACKET_SAFELIST to prevent scaffolding bleed-through, and the retry hint now carries top-3 specific issues instead of 'structurally empty'. The final-attempt accept path is a defensible starvation-vs-strictness tradeoff. Aggregate should move from 3.06 → ~3.73 (well on track for 4.0 by iter 3-4). Top remaining lifts in priority order: (a) per-dimension 1/3/5 anchors for the 8 scoring dimensions (strategy rq-local-002, pure prose in agent.py — highest ROI remaining), (b) journey_stage enum in review-output-schema.json (trivial, rq-local-005), (c) business-goal token-overlap check in validate_review_output (rq-local-003 partial, cheap), (d) persona_voice / finding-field non-empty checks inside validate_review_output to formalize what the post-filter already implies.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Add a '## Scoring Rubric' block to build_review_packet (after '## Scoring Dimensions', before '## Voice Check'). For each of the 8 dimensions (task_clarity, task_success, effort_load, trust_confidence, value_communication, error_recovery, accessibility, emotional_fit), provide a one-line definition of what the dimension measures from the persona's perspective and concrete anchors for score 1 (failing), 3 (adequate), and 5 (excellent) — tied to the persona's core_journey and business_goal. This resolves central-tendency collapse by converting 'what feels right' into 'which row matches the evidence I cited in the reason field'. Also enforce reason-before-score ordering in the schema template so chain-of-thought precedes the numeric commit (the schema currently emits reason and score together; make the LLM write reason first).

Extend _check_evidence_grounding to also cover strengths[].evidence (currently only findings are scanned) and scores[dim].reason if the reason quotes specific copy. Optionally widen the window check from a strict trigram to a 3-gram OR a quoted-span match (detect content between straight/curly quotes and require that the quoted span itself exists in webpage_context) — this catches the common hallucination pattern 'the hero says "Sign up free"' where the LLM fabricates a direct quote. Add a one-line log warning (stderr or raw_output sidecar) when the final-attempt accept path ships with unresolved semantic_issues so reviewers can audit the soft-accept cases.

Add a token-overlap check in run_review: for each entry in prioritized_improvements.quick_wins / structural_fixes / validation_experiments, verify that expected_business_outcome shares at least one content token with brief.business_goal (exclude stopwords: the/a/an/to/and/or/of/in/for/with/on). Thread brief.business_goal into run_review alongside the existing webpage_context/allowed_competitors kwargs. Surface token-overlap failures through the same semantic_issues retry channel so the model receives specific feedback ('quick_win #2 expected_business_outcome does not mention the stated business goal').

Two lightweight follow-ups: (1) extend the '## Voice Check' block to also cover review_summary.first_impression and review_summary.why_it_matters — these are first-person-heavy fields currently outside the voice reinforcement; (2) add a tiny code-level voice probe that checks each finding.persona_voice for at least one of persona.voice[:5] (case-insensitive substring), surfacing failures as semantic_issues. Optional: replace the generic service.py:119-134 fallback persona voice anchors with a 'FALLBACK PERSONA — voice unavailable' marker so downstream reviewers aren't misled by synthetic-but-plausible anchors.

Add a lightweight blocker-cap enforcement in the semantic post-check: if len([f for f in findings if f.priority=='Blocker']) > max(1, len(findings)//5), raise a semantic_issue asking the model to downgrade the weakest Blockers. Also spot-check that each finding's impact_on_user AND impact_on_business are both populated strings (not empty, not one is a copy of the other) — this formalizes the iter-1 Nielsen-triad rubric at code level without requiring triad parsing.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iter 2 is a narrow, surgical improvement. The post-response semantic validation (evidence grounding + competitor leak detection) is the headline change and it's well engineered: it closes a real reliability gap — structurally valid but semantically ungrounded or leak-prone responses now loop back with targeted retry notes, and the last-attempt bypass prevents infinite-loop starvation. Combined with the threaded webpage_context + allowed_competitors in service.py, this raises retry_reliability from 4.5 to 4.75. The Voice Check / voice audit additions in agent.py are real quality content at modest token cost. HOWEVER, none of the four priority_fixes from iter 1's memo (embedded-schema deletion, duplicate webpage fetch, competitor-rule consolidation, schema score-field reorder) were addressed — these remain the highest-leverage remaining wins. The single change that would most move aggregate score is still: delete the JSON schema block at agent.py:266-268 (already attached via responseSchema in 2/3 builders) and replace with a one-line key list. This is ~500-800 tokens/call reclaimed, removes a Google-documented anti-pattern, and lifts both llm_configuration (→4.0+) and token_efficiency. Second-highest-leverage: add @functools.lru_cache to fetch_webpage_context to eliminate the duplicate crawl. Third: fix review-output-schema.json to {reason, score} order — a one-line edit with measurable CoT benefit.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Delete the embedded JSON schema block at agent.py:265-268 (`## Required Output Schema` + ```json + json.dumps(schema) + ```). Replace with a one-line reference: `Return JSON with these top-level keys: review_summary, persona_card, scores, strengths, findings, prioritized_improvements, open_questions.` The schema is already attached as responseSchema in build_request_inline_prompt (gemini.py:430) and build_request_plain_text_fallback (gemini.py:462). Google's controlled-generation docs explicitly warn that duplicating the schema in both the prompt and responseSchema 'might lead to lower-quality generated output.' This change alone reclaims ~500-800 tokens per call and removes a documented anti-pattern. For the grounded builder (which can't set responseSchema alongside google_search), the one-line key list is sufficient structure guidance given responseMimeType=application/json is already set.

Eliminate the duplicate fetch_webpage_context call across persona + review stages. Cheapest fix: decorate fetch_webpage_context at webpage.py:113 with `@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=128)` — per-process memoization by URL makes the second call a no-op. Cleaner fix: in service.py, call fetch_webpage_context once in generate_persona_from_form (service.py:90) and thread that crawl into build_packet_for_brief via a new optional `webpage_context` parameter so build_packet_for_brief skips re-crawling. Either change cuts one full 8-page crawl per end-to-end request and removes ~4.4k duplicate input tokens on the persona stage.

Three small consolidations: (1) Reorder `review-output-schema.json` score fields from `{score, reason}` to `{reason, score}` at lines 24-31 — this is a one-file edit that materially improves CoT for the 8 score fields (reasoning-before-commit is a documented best practice). (2) Delete the SYSTEM_PROMPT re-embedding at agent.py:169-170 — it's already in systemInstruction (gemini.py:397-405) for the grounded builder, and the inline/fallback builders have their own terse preambles; reduces prompt duplication and improves caching prefix stability. (3) Consolidate the competitor rule to ONE location (SYSTEM_PROMPT at agent.py:46-52) — delete restatements at agent.py:195 (Known Competitors header), gemini.py:472 (plain-text fallback), and gemini.py:521-526 (enrich_persona). Repetition does not strengthen instruction following; it dilutes attention.

Two small polish items only — the retry loop is otherwise near best-practice. (1) On semantic-validation failure, rotate to a different REQUEST_BUILDER for the next attempt rather than reusing the same builder_idx (gemini.py:225) — the three builder shapes have different structural characteristics (e.g., builder 1 runs google_search which can pull in unapproved names causing competitor_leak flags; switching to builder 2 on a leak would be smarter than re-running builder 1 with a warning). (2) Extend `_parse_retry_after` at gemini.py:54-60 to also parse HTTP-date form (RFC 7231); `float(value)` only handles the delta-seconds variant. Both are incremental; retry_reliability is already 4.75.

evaluator_feedback.feedback: Iteration 2 delivers one focused, correct fix: prefers-reduced-motion fallbacks now cover every keyframe in the app (WCAG SC 2.3.3). The attribute selector `[style*="spin"]` is a clever way to reach the inline spinner animation without refactoring the inline-style. However, five out of six priority fixes from iter 1 are still open after two iterations: (1) bilingual propagation into render_persona_card/render_result is the single largest remaining WCAG+UX win and affects both accessibility and i18n_bilingual simultaneously; (2) Regenerate button still calls `location.reload()` instead of re-POSTing /persona — this is user-facing broken functionality; (3) the health poller still hammers /health every 1.5s on hidden tabs and hard-reloads on recovery; (4) semantic landmarks and aria-live regions are still absent; (5) the required-field `*` indicator is still missing. With 3 iterations left to hit the 4.2 target, the fixer should bundle multiple quick accessibility wins per iteration rather than shipping one keyframe guard per cycle.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Propagate bilingual support into render_persona_card (L719-866) and render_result (L482-716): (a) replace static `<html lang="en">` at L593 and L739 with the active language read from `localStorage['qra-lang']`; (b) add data-en/data-ko attributes to all user-facing strings on those two templates (step indicator, button labels, section headings, 'Run another review', etc.); (c) duplicate the on-load setLang() block so the preference is applied on persona and result pages too. Fixes WCAG SC 3.1.1 / 3.1.2 and unblocks i18n_bilingual simultaneously.

Add semantic landmarks to all three templates: wrap the form body in `<main>` (inside render_form, around L361), wrap the persona card in `<main role="main">` (render_persona_card), wrap the result in `<main>` with a `<header>` for the hero and `<section aria-labelledby>` per review section. Add `role="status" aria-live="polite"` to the AUTO_RECONNECT_SCRIPT banner (L24-39) and `role="alert"` to the error div at L185. Add `aria-pressed` to the two lang-switch buttons at L355-356, toggled inside setLang(). Fixes WCAG SC 1.3.1 and 4.1.3.

Fix the Regenerate button at L834. Replace `onclick="location.reload()"` with an addEventListener handler that POSTs the stashed original form fields (already present as hidden inputs L824-833) back to /persona with a fresh nonce, shows the skeleton, and swaps in the new persona card. Currently the button is a no-op from the user's perspective. Also add visible `*` required indicators to the 5 required inputs (service_name, service_url, service_type, core_journey, persona_description) and expose aria-busy+disabled on the submit button during the /persona round-trip.

In AUTO_RECONNECT_SCRIPT (L18-66), pause the 1.5s /health poller when `document.hidden === true` via a visibilitychange listener, resume on 'visible'. Replace the unconditional `window.location.reload()` on recovery at L49 with a soft recovery: update banner text to 'Reconnected' and aria-live-announce, only force-reload if the server reports a stale session. Also: add progressive phase text to render_skeleton (L1097+) that updates at 0s/5s/15s/30s in both EN and KO, announced via the same `role='status' aria-live='polite'` region.

Confidence pill at L669 uses `background:{conf_color}18` (~10% alpha) with the same color for text — non-text contrast of the pill edge against the panel is likely below WCAG 1.4.11's 3:1 requirement. Either darken the background alpha (e.g., `{conf_color}33` = 20%) AND verify the text-on-fill contrast, or add a 1px border `border:1px solid {conf_color}` to establish the 3:1 edge. Also convert `body { font-size:14px }` at L601 to `1rem` (or `0.875rem` if truly 14px is desired but expressed in rem) for user font-scaling.

Rationalize the three breakpoints (860px L342, 768px L656, 720px L772) into a single token system. Either adopt a unified `min-width` mobile-first approach — default single-column, `@media (min-width: 640px)` two-column, `@media (min-width: 1024px)` three-column — or document a content-driven reason for each existing value. Current desktop-first max-width queries with three different thresholds create inconsistent layouts between 720-860px viewport widths.

strategy: iteration 2 gains: evidence_grounding 2.5 → 4.25 (+1.75, webpage_context threading + _check_evidence_grounding + _check_competitor_leak delivered), persona_grounding_and_voice 2.0 → 4.0 (+2.0, voice carry-through + reflection-loop voice audit delivered), retry_reliability 4.5 → 4.75 (+0.25, semantic-failure retry with targeted notes), loading_feedback 3.0 → 3.5 (+0.5, prefers-reduced-motion fallbacks delivered)

iteration 2 gaps: scoring_rubric_calibration (2.5, no 1/3/5 anchors for 8 dimensions — highest-ROI remaining review_quality lift), embedded schema anti-pattern persists in agent.py (flagged 3× now, ~500-800 tokens/call wasted), duplicate webpage fetch persists (2 full crawls per request), webapp.py bilingual propagation entirely absent (affects accessibility + i18n_bilingual simultaneously), Regenerate button still a no-op (location.reload instead of re-POST)

iteration 3 priorities: (1) scoring rubric 1/3/5 anchors in agent.py — pure prose, zero structural risk, lifts scoring_rubric_calibration from 2.5 toward 4.0; (2) delete embedded schema from packet — 1-line change, reclaims tokens, lifts llm_configuration; (3) bilingual propagation into render_persona_card + render_result — lifts accessibility from 3.0 and i18n_bilingual from 2.5 simultaneously; (4) Regenerate button re-POST fix — user-facing broken functionality, webapp.py L834; (5) lru_cache on fetch_webpage_context — removes duplicate crawl, lifts pipeline_structure

applied_fixes.description: Threaded webpage_context and brief.competitors through run_review; added _check_evidence_grounding (3-word sliding-window substring match with JS-unobservable escape hatch) and _check_competitor_leak (grammatical multi-word title-case regex with packet-scaffolding safelist and word-boundary context matching). On semantic failure build targeted Retry Note; last attempt bypasses to avoid loop starvation.

Inserted '## Voice Check (do not skip)' block immediately before Execution Instructions referencing persona.name and persona.voice[:5]; added 'Voice audit' bullet to Reflection Loop requiring first-person rewriting of auditor-voice sentences.
applied_fixes.effect: persona_grounding_and_voice 3.0 -> 4.0 (+1.0). Critic confirmed vs arxiv 2511.00222 and Medium stunspot persona-prompting article.

wisdom_source.strategy_name: Voice Carry-Through Reinforcement Near Execution Instructions

applied_fixes.description: Added @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) fallbacks to both style blocks: the spin keyframe area (~L452) and the skeleton_css block footer (~L1085) covering all four keyframes (spin, skel-shimmer, skel-fade-in, skel-dot-anim) plus [class*='shimmer'] and [class*='skel-'] pattern matchers.
applied_fixes.effect: loading_feedback 3.0 -> 3.5 (+0.5). WCAG SC 2.3.3 compliant. accessibility held flat because the dominant items (landmarks, aria, lang propagation) remain open.
